name: Hordaland (Norway, 1961–2019)

colors.symbolism: the field of the county arms

description: A red field charged with three gold battle-axes meeting at the centre in a pall arrangement, blades outward.
history: Granted to Hordaland county on 12 May 1961. The arms derive from a medieval seal. The county was abolished on 1 January 2020 when it was merged with Sogn og Fjordane into the new Vestland county.

features.symbolism: heraldic banner of the county arms: three gold battle-axes in pall on a red field, taken from a medieval seal of the Hordaland province
three Olav-axes meeting at the centre, drawn from a medieval seal associated with the historic Hordaland law-province
